facilities.title: Vida Senior Care
facilities.editorial:
Vida Senior Care occupies a shophouse at 59 Jalan Wawasan 3/5, Pusat Bandar Puchong — in the commercial hub of Puchong town, with easy access from the Lebuhraya Damansara-Puchong (LDP) and the Puchong-Kinrara road network. The facility is categorised across three care tiers: aged care, assisted living, and nursing home — a range that suggests the home accepts residents across varying dependency levels, from lower-acuity individuals who need daily assistance through to those requiring full nursing oversight. The Google Business profile indicates that Vida employs a combination of on-duty doctors and nursing staff.

The 4.8-star Google rating from 20 reviews is strong for a shophouse-format nursing home in the Puchong market. The shophouse premises is worth considering from a physical access perspective: converted shophouses can be well suited for ambulatory residents, but families with a relative who is bedbound or uses a wheelchair should confirm that the layout and bathroom configuration are appropriate before committing. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry.

When you contact Vida Senior Care, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and current validity, the total bed count and current occupancy, and whether the on-duty doctor is present on a scheduled daily basis or available on-call. Ask about nurse-to-resident staffing ratios for overnight shifts, which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, bedbound residents — and how clinical deterioration is escalated. Confirm the monthly pricing model and whether medication, physiotherapy and incontinence supplies are included in the base fee or itemised separately.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan En Yuan (Cheras)
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan En Yuan is located at 5 Jalan 28, Kampung Cheras Baru — in the terrace-house nursing home corridor on the Selangor-KL border along the Cheras-Kajang axis. The facility has a disclosed bed count of 50, placing it firmly in the medium-sized category for a Klang Valley nursing home where most terrace-format facilities operate at 20 to 40 beds. The operator's Mandarin name — En Yuan, broadly meaning grace and affinity — signals a Chinese-community character, consistent with the Cheras Baru cluster's demographic orientation. The Cheras-Kajang Expressway gives the location reasonable access from both Cheras and the inner KL ring.

The 4.2-star Google rating from 20 reviews is a competent mid-range result — not a flag in either direction, but suggesting a mixed pool of experiences. At 50 beds, this home is large enough that staffing consistency across shifts is an important variable to probe carefully. The Kampung Cheras Baru block hosts several nursing homes within close proximity of each other, giving visiting families the practical advantage of comparing multiple facilities in one trip before deciding.

When you contact Pusat Jagaan En Yuan, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and current status, confirmation of the actual capacity and current occupancy across all floors, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. At 50 beds, the overnight nurse count is a particularly important question: regulatory minimums and best practice diverge meaningfully at this scale. Ask which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, post-stroke bedbound residents — and confirm the monthly pricing structure and what is included in the base fee. A visit to see the premises, including bedroom size and bathroom accessibility, is strongly recommended.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Mahmudah Malaysia
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Mahmudah Malaysia is a registered welfare care centre at Lot 459/5, Jalan 1 Off Jalan Bangi Lama, Kampung Sungai Purun, Semenyih, Selangor — a rural-fringe township in the Hulu Langat district, approximately 30 to 35 km from central Kuala Lumpur. The listing metadata describes the location simply as Selangor, but the actual address is in the Semenyih corridor rather than any major urban nursing home cluster. The centre was established in 2016 and is registered under the Care Centres Act 1993 with Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat, operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Mahmudah Malaysia — a welfare organisation rather than a private commercial entity.

Being operated by a welfare association under JKM registration shapes the intake and funding model significantly. This is not a fee-paying private nursing home in the conventional sense; admission is managed through welfare criteria and JKM processes rather than a standard commercial application. The 4.5-star Google rating from 20 reviews is solid for a welfare-registered facility in a rural Selangor location. Families exploring a private commercial placement should note that this home operates on a different framework from licensed private nursing homes.

For families or social workers supporting a relative in or near the Semenyih-Hulu Langat area who may qualify under welfare eligibility criteria, the correct first step is to contact Persatuan Kebajikan Mahmudah Malaysia directly to understand the intake process and current capacity. Questions to ask include: what the eligibility criteria are, whether JKM referral is required, what JKM licence type and current status applies, and what level of nursing care is available on-site. Families seeking a private paid placement in the Semenyih corridor should look at licensed commercial nursing homes in the area instead.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan is a Muslim welfare home for the elderly operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor (PKNENJ), a registered NGO active since November 2008. The home is located in Kempas Baru, Johor Bahru — a growing residential district in south-central JB. This is not a commercial nursing home: it is a faith-based welfare facility that accepts destitute, abandoned and family-less elderly Muslims, individuals with physical and mental disabilities, and converts to Islam (muallaf). Residents are typically referred through JKM officers or hospital social work units rather than through direct family applications.

The welfare mandate defines both who can access this home and how the intake process works. Families with an elderly relative who has family support and the financial means for a private placement are outside the home's target population; Nur Ehsan exists specifically for those who have no family support system and no income. The 4.3-star Google rating from 19 reviews reflects steady community appreciation — likely from volunteers, donors and welfare workers familiar with the organisation's work. Pricing is welfare and donation-based with JKM subsidy accepted, not a commercial monthly rate.

For JKM officers, hospital social workers or community members supporting an elderly Muslim individual with no family or financial resources in Johor Bahru, Nur Ehsan is a relevant referral. The intake route typically runs through JKM rather than direct family contact — a social welfare officer or hospital social work team can facilitate the referral process. For families with a relative who needs a private nursing home placement in the JB Kempas corridor, the appropriate referral is one of the licensed commercial facilities operating in the same area.

facilities.title: St. Mary's Nursing Home (PJ Old Town)
facilities.editorial:
St. Mary's Nursing Home PJ Old Town is at No. 22, Jalan Othman, Petaling Jaya — in PJ Old Town's established residential grid, a short distance from Jalan Gasing and within the central PJ catchment. It is one branch of St. Mary's Nursing & Aged Home Sdn. Bhd., a brand founded in 1985 by Mdm Sonia, a UK-trained nurse, making it one of the longest-running private elder care names in the Klang Valley. Four decades of continuous operation give St. Mary's a track record and institutional credibility that newer facilities cannot match.

The PJ Old Town branch holds a 4.9-star Google rating from 19 reviews — among the stronger scores in the PJ corridor for homes in this review band. The founder's professional nursing background and the long operating history underpin the clinical culture at St. Mary's in a way that is worth noting when families are comparing newer commercial entrants. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry. The St. Mary's brand operates multiple branches, so confirming which specific sites currently have availability — and whether staffing and care standards are consistent across branches — is worth raising on your initial enquiry.

When you contact St. Mary's PJ Old Town, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number for this specific branch, the current number of residents and bed capacity, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Ask which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, post-stroke bedbound residents — and how clinical escalation is handled after hours. Confirm the monthly pricing structure and what the base fee covers. As a multi-branch operator with four decades of history, St. Mary's is one of the better-known independent nursing home names in central PJ, and a visit to the Jalan Othman premises before placement is recommended.

facilities.title: Woon Ho Family Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Woon Ho Family Care Centre operates from Lot 3468, Jalan Lurah 23, Kempas Baru, Johor Bahru — in the same south-central JB nursing home corridor as several other private facilities in the area. The home spans three care tiers: assisted living for lower-dependency residents, skilled nursing for those who need clinical oversight, and rehabilitation services. Published amenities include 24-hour nursing coverage, four meals per day, daily recreational activities, and a physiotherapy programme. Doctor consultations are noted in the operator's listing.

The most practically useful data point for families comparing JB options is the published starting price: from RM 2,000 per month. This places Woon Ho at the accessible end of the JB private nursing home spectrum — below the RM 3,200+ starting points of the EHA Eldercare network — and broadly competitive with community-level nursing homes in the Kempas corridor. The 5.0-star Google rating from 19 reviews is consistent. Whether that clean score reflects consistently strong care or a primarily referral-driven review base is worth verifying through your own visit.

When you contact Woon Ho Family Care Centre, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and status, the total number of beds and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Clarify whether physiotherapy is provided by an in-house qualified physiotherapist or an external contractor, and ask on what schedule the visiting doctor calls. Confirm the full pricing model: whether RM 2,000 is the entry-level shared-room rate, what additional tiers exist, and which services — medication, incontinence supplies, specialist visits — are included versus itemised separately. A site visit to inspect the rooms and common areas is recommended before committing.

facilities.title: Pristine Nursing Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Pristine Nursing Care Centre is located in Bandar Puchong Jaya, Puchong — a mature township south of Puchong proper, characterised by shophouse rows and landed residential estates. The facility is also listed as 'Pristine Senior Care Centre' on its Facebook presence, and describes itself as providing care and support for seniors and physically disabled individuals in a warm, supportive environment. The care type is listed as nursing home. Beyond a Google Maps listing and a Facebook page, the home does not maintain a detailed public website, so most service information needs to be gathered through direct contact.

The 4.3-star Google rating from 19 reviews is a steady mid-market result for a Puchong-area nursing home — not exceptional, but without an obvious pattern of concern. The Bandar Puchong Jaya location is accessible for families in the Puchong-Bukit Jalil-Sri Petaling corridor and is reasonably reached via the Lebuhraya Damansara-Puchong and Puchong-Kinrara road network. Pricing is not published and is quoted on enquiry. Founding year, bed capacity and JKM licence details are not available from public listings.

When you contact Pristine Nursing Care Centre, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and its current validity status. Ask about the total bed count and current occupancy, the nurse-to-resident ratio for day and overnight shifts, and which care conditions are accepted — assisted living, nursing care, dementia, palliative, bedbound residents. Confirm the monthly pricing structure and whether medication, incontinence supplies and physiotherapy are included in the base fee or billed separately. Also ask whether the home accepts both male and female residents, as not all smaller care homes do. A site visit before making a placement decision is recommended.

facilities.title: Love and Care Centre Kajang
facilities.editorial:
Love and Care Centre Kajang is at No. 1, Jalan S8, Taman Sakap, 43000 Kajang — a quiet residential area in the southern Klang Valley, approximately 30 km from KL city centre. The centre is a ministry of social concern operated by the Lutheran Church of Malaysia and was established in 2003, initially as a women-only home, with a male section added in 2006. It is registered with the Selangor Social Welfare Department. The faith-based character of this centre is central to how it operates: this is a welfare home founded on Lutheran Christian values rather than a medically-oriented commercial nursing facility.

The welfare and faith-based model shapes both the admission process and the resident experience. Families seeking a private fee-paying commercial placement with full nursing care, scheduled doctor visits and clinical rehabilitation services are looking at a different category of facility. Love and Care Centre serves elderly individuals within a faith community and social welfare context; fees, if applicable, are structured differently from commercial nursing homes. The 3.8-star Google rating from 18 reviews sits below the market average — in a welfare home context, this often reflects reviewers from outside the served community whose expectations were misaligned with the home's actual mandate and character.

For families connected to the Lutheran community in Kajang, or for individuals in the Taman Sakap area who may qualify under the home's welfare criteria, a direct call is the right first step. Ask about the current admission process and eligibility criteria, what fee structure (if any) applies, what level of nursing and medical care is available on-site, and whether the home is registered under the Akta Pusat Jagaan or a different welfare regulatory framework. For families seeking a fully licensed private nursing home in the Kajang area, the appropriate referral is a commercial facility operating under the Akta Pusat Jagaan.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Husna Arrashid
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Husna Arrashid is a Muslim-oriented private nursing home at No. 14B, Lorong Kijang, Kampung Kelang Gates Baharu, 53300 Kuala Lumpur — in the Setapak-Wangsa Maju corridor of northern KL. The centre operates under an Islamic care framework, maintains its own website, and was first registered with the Selangor Social Welfare Department in December 2016, indicating several years of operational development before achieving formal registration. Families should confirm the current regulatory jurisdiction — JKM KL or Selangor — and verify the registration status at the time of enquiry.

The 3.6-star Google rating from 18 reviews sits below the typical private nursing home average, which in the KL-Selangor market tends to cluster between 4.0 and 4.7. The sample of 18 reviews is meaningful enough to reflect an accumulated pattern of experiences rather than isolated incidents. A rating at this level can arise from a range of factors — physical facility constraints, staffing, management responsiveness or communication issues — and the way to understand the specifics is through a direct visit and conversations with both care staff and families of current residents. Families considering this home are advised to use that information as a prompt for thorough in-person assessment before committing.

When you visit Pusat Jagaan Husna Arrashid, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and current expiry date, the total number of beds and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Ask which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, bedbound residents — and request to meet the care manager. Speaking with one or two families of current residents, if the home can arrange it, is worth requesting. Pricing is quoted on direct enquiry. For Muslim families in the Wangsa Maju-Setapak corridor seeking an Islamic care environment, this home is one of the few options in the immediate neighbourhood.

facilities.title: RS Global Home Care (Muslim Only)
facilities.editorial:
RS Global Home Care, at 1C Jalan 2, Kampung Cheras Baru, Kuala Lumpur, is a residential nursing home serving Muslim residents exclusively. The home's name is explicit about its eligibility criteria, and its care model integrates Islamic practices into the daily routine alongside conventional residential nursing. Reviewers consistently highlight the Islamic programming as a key differentiator: residents receive assistance with daily prayers, participate in Quran recitation sessions, and are served exclusively halal meals. An extensive photo gallery on Google Maps indicates active community engagement and a resident population that appears regularly photographed at group activities.

For Muslim families in the Cheras-Pandan corridor seeking a care environment that goes beyond halal food service to actively structure the daily rhythm around Islamic practice, RS Global Home Care is one of the more clearly positioned options in the Klang Valley market. The 4.7-star Google rating from 18 reviews is strong. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry. The Kampung Cheras Baru location on the KL side of the Cheras corridor is accessible from Pandan, Ampang and the wider Cheras township.

When you contact RS Global Home Care, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and expiry date, the total bed count and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Confirm which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, post-stroke rehabilitation, bedbound residents — and how clinical escalation is handled after hours given the residential location. Ask about the daily Islamic activity programme in detail: prayer assistance, Quran sessions, dietary provisions, and how residents who observe different practices are accommodated. Confirm the monthly pricing structure and what the base fee includes.
